age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 55 years as of the Age Cut-off Date (31-05-2026). Age relaxation will be as per government rules.

qualification.education_qualification: Post Graduate/MBA/PGDM/Chartered Accountant (CA)/Company Secretary (CS)/Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA)/Financial Risk Manager (FRM) from recognized university/institute, Professional certification in compliance related field will be an added advantage
qualification.details: Post Graduate/MBA/PGDM/Chartered Accountant (CA)/Company Secretary (CS)/Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA)/Financial Risk Manager (FRM) or equivalent from a recognized university/institute. A professional certification in a compliance-related field will be an added advantage.

how_to_apply: Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Download the application form from the official website: https://www.iifcl.in.
Fill the application form carefully with correct information.
Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates.
Check all details carefully before submission.
Send the completed application form to the address mentioned in the notification through Speed Post / Registered Post or as instructed.
Ensure the application reaches before the last date: 10-07-2026.
Keep a photocopy of the submitted application form for future reference.

all_official_links.label: Download IIFCL Chief Compliance Officer (CCOM) Job 2026 Notification PDF

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the IIFCL Chief Compliance Officer post?
faq.answer: The last date to receive the application for the Chief Compliance Officer (CCOM) position at IIFCL is 10th July 2026. Ensure your application reaches the designated address before this date.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for the Chief Compliance Officer role?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a Post Graduate degree, MBA, PGDM, or be a Chartered Accountant (CA), Company Secretary (CS), Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA), or Financial Risk Manager (FRM) from a recognized institution. A professional certification in compliance is preferred.

faq.question: Q3. What is the maximum age limit for applicants?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for applicants is 55 years as of the age cut-off date, which is 31st May 2026. Age relaxation will be provided as per government norms.

faq.question: Q4. How will candidates be selected for the Chief Compliance Officer position?
faq.answer: The selection process for the Chief Compliance Officer (CCOM) role at IIFCL involves shortlisting of eligible candidates followed by an interview. Performance in both stages will determine the final selection.

faq.question: Q5. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for the Chief Compliance Officer (CCOM) recruitment at IIFCL. Candidates can apply without paying any fee.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary offered for the Chief Compliance Officer post?
faq.answer: The selected candidate for the Chief Compliance Officer (CCOM) position will receive a consolidated salary of Rs. 4,50,000 per month, which is an all-inclusive Cost to Company (CTC).

faq.question: Q7. What is the mode of application for this IIFCL recruitment?
faq.answer: The application for the Chief Compliance Officer (CCOM) post in IIFCL recruitment is to be submitted offline. Candidates need to download the form, fill it, and send it via post.

faq.question: Q8. How many vacancies are available for the Chief Compliance Officer post?
faq.answer: There is a total of 1 vacancy for the post of Chief Compliance Officer (CCOM) in the India Infrastructure Finance Company Limited (IIFCL) recruitment 2026.

faq.question: Q9. Where can I download the application form?
faq.answer: The application form can be downloaded from the official website of India Infrastructure Finance Company Limited (IIFCL), which is https://www.iifcl.in.

faq.question: Q10. What documents should be attached with the application form?
faq.answer: Candidates should attach self-attested copies of required documents, including a photograph, signature, ID proof, and relevant educational and professional certificates, as specified in the official notification.
